tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Iran formally rejected the US 15-point peace plan on Mar 26, calling it 'maximalist and unreasonable' (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg); Brent rebounded to ~$101 (WTI $90.26 confirmed) as peace-plan de-escalation trade fully unwound. Iran's 5 counter-conditions include Hormuz sovereignty - structurally incompatible with US terms. Tail risk raised to 33%. The $98-101 zone was the re-entry opportunity; PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta. Reduce conviction only on confirmed Pakistan-hosted ceasefire framework with full commercial Hormuz reopening.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Iran formally rejected US peace plan on Mar 26 (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg); Brent rebounded to ~$101 (WTI $90.26 confirmed); iTraxx Asia IG est. ~172bp (+4bp). ASEAN Energy Equities est. +2.1% tracking oil rebound; ASEAN Banks -2.5% as spreads widen on rejection. Energy-over-banks pair confirmed: peace-plan de-escalation trade fully unwound; pair trade re-enters from stronger footing. Trim banks underweight only if iTraxx breaks below 155bp on confirmed Pakistan-hosted ceasefire.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short - Dip Opportunity on Trump-Driven Rally
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP est. 60.10 (risk-off reversal; Day 25 PHP strength on peace plan fully unwound by Iran rejection Mar 26). Brent ~$101 and JKM ~$21.20/MMBtu (est.) - Philippines LNG import costs re-escalating. Iran rejected US peace plan; demanded Hormuz sovereignty (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Mar 26); no Ras Laffan restart path. PHP re-entry on the $59.75 dip was confirmed; stress target 61.50-62.50 remains. Stop only on confirmed ceasefire and Ras Laffan restart.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short - Re-Entry on Trump Pause Dip
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR est. 17,100 (risk-off reversal; Day 25 IDR strength on peace plan fully unwound by Iran rejection Mar 26). Brent ~$101 now $31/bbl above Indonesia's $70 ICP budget assumption; ID 10Y est. 6.90% (+5bp), Bank Indonesia constrained. Iran rejected US peace plan; demanded Hormuz sovereignty (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Mar 26). IDR re-entry on 16,800-17,000 dip confirmed; stress target 18,000 activated on rejection - next inflection is Pakistan-hosted talks outcome.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection - Hold Through Mar 28
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG est. ~172bp (+4bp, Mar 26); ASEAN HY est. ~522bp (+12bp). Iran formally rejected US 15-point plan; demanded Hormuz sovereignty - P&I insurance restoration now structurally impossible without a binding political settlement (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Mar 26). Lebanon front re-activated; Israel ops continuing. Day 25 peace-plan CDS tightening fully reversed. 185-200bp remains the next stress target if Pakistan-hosted talks fail or Trump resumes power plant strikes. Hold CDS protection.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold Through Mar 28 Deadline
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM ~$21.20/MMBtu (est., +3.3%, Mar 26); TTF ~€58.00/MWh (est., +5.5%, Mar 26). Iran formally rejected US peace plan and demanded Hormuz sovereignty (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Mar 26) - Qatar Ras Laffan restart now structurally blocked without a political settlement. The add-on-rejection-signal moment has arrived: JKM is spicing back toward $23+ while TTF's European gas response remains less acute. JKM-over-TTF spread long confirmed; hold and add. Stop only on confirmed Ras Laffan restart.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window remains structurally closed. iTraxx Asia IG est. ~172bp; ASEAN HY est. ~522bp - unsecured market inaccessible. Iran rejected US peace plan on Mar 26 (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg); P&I insurance blackout continues. Tail scenario probability raised to 33%. Activate secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures immediately - Pakistan-hosted talks are the only remaining exit and timeline is uncertain.

PTTEP should pre-fund before THB window closes - TH 10Y est. 2.05% (+3bp, risk-off), USD/THB est. 33.050 (THB weakening on Iran rejection). Brent ~$101 (est., Mar 26) boosts upstream EBITDA vs $70 pre-shock; JKM ~$21.20/MMBtu (est.) keeps gas import costs elevated. Iran rejected US peace plan and demanded Hormuz sovereignty (Al Jazeera, Mar 26) - Gulf JV compliance review is now more urgent. Execute H2 2026 prefunding and complete Gulf JV sanctions review before Pakistan-hosted talks begin.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity immediately. Brent ~$101 and JKM ~$21.20 (est.) re-escalating vs pre-shock; USD/SGD est. 1.2780 (mild USD strengthening on risk-off). Iran rejected US peace plan; demanded Hormuz sovereignty (Al Jazeera, Mar 26) - P&I insurance blackout and Ras Laffan shutdown structurally locked until political settlement. Day 25 de-escalation window has closed; execute revolving credit now before spreads widen further toward 185bp iTraxx IG.

Hyflux must activate restructuring immediately. Base 15%, stress 52%, tail 33%; Brent ~$101 (est.); ASEAN HY est. ~522bp. JKM ~$21.20 (est.) spikes gas-fired desalination costs. Iran rejected US peace plan and demanded Hormuz sovereignty on Mar 26 (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg) - tail scenario (oil $160+, LNG markets seized) at 33% probability. Pakistan-hosted talks are only remaining exit; timeline uncertain. Unsecured market closed; pursue private-credit and quasi-sovereign structures with urgency.
